title: The Rosetta Stone: Decree of Memphis
description: The Rosetta Stone — the trilingual decree that unlocked the Egyptian hieroglyphs. Written in hieroglyphic, Demotic, and Greek, it allowed Jean-Francois Champollion to decipher the ancient Egyptian writing system in 1822, opening 3,000 years of history to modern understanding.
introduction:
The Rosetta Stone is arguably the most important archaeological discovery in history. Found by Napoleon's soldiers in 1799 near the town of Rashid (Rosetta) in the Nile Delta, this granodiorite stele bears a single decree written in three scripts: Egyptian hieroglyphs, Demotic script, and ancient Greek. Because scholars could read the Greek, they could work backward to decipher the hieroglyphs — a writing system that had been unreadable for 1,400 years.

Jean-Francois Champollion announced his decipherment in 1822, unlocking the entire literary, religious, and historical record of ancient Egypt. Without the Rosetta Stone, the Pyramid Texts, the Book of the Dead, the Tale of Sinuhe, and every other Egyptian text on this site would still be unreadable symbols.

translation.text: In the reign of the young one who has received the kingship from his father, lord of crowns, glorious, who has established Egypt and is pious towards the gods, superior to his foes, who has restored the civilized life of men, lord of the Thirty-Year Festivals like Hephaestus the Great, a king like the Sun, great king of the upper and lower countries, offspring of the Gods who love the Father, whom Hephaestus has approved, to whom the Sun has given the victory: the living image of Zeus, son of the Sun, Ptolemy, living forever, beloved of Ptah. In the ninth year, when Aetus son of Aetus was priest of Alexander — the gods decreed that this stone be set up in every temple of the first, second, and third rank, beside the image of the king.
